what_it_does: Defines a static ConceptV design-system specimen page for tonal zoning, rendering a zone registry, component-role examples, an applied Overview dashboard mock, a legend, responsive scaling behavior, and a React-based tweaks panel for tint, palette, gold, and spatial settings.
observations: The page links local CSS files for color/type and tonal-zoning styles.
The masthead explains near-white tinted surfaces and reserves gold for Vi, active decisions, selected states, and emphasis.
A TZ_ZONES array defines ten semantic zones with key, role, name, pigment token, tint percentage, and description.
The zone registry and applied legend are built imperatively from JavaScript data rather than hard-coded markup.
The applied Overview mock includes left navigation, central dashboard content, source/provenance activity, and a right Vi rail.
Inline SVG is used for spatial path lines, nodes, and flow markers.
The Overview mock is designed at a fixed width and scaled to fit its frame with ResizeObserver.
React, ReactDOM, and Babel are loaded from pinned CDN URLs, while tweaks-panel.jsx is loaded locally.
The tweaks panel writes CSS custom properties and body classes to alter tint intensity, gold intensity, palette, and spatial opacity.
standouts: Semantic surfaces are represented as named pigment-to-white zones with visible swatches and token labels.
The same zoning vocabulary is shown both as a registry and as an applied dashboard composition.
A live tweaks panel exposes the design language as adjustable parameters rather than a static specimen.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties as the main contract between HTML, generated DOM, and visual styling.
Uses semantic class prefixes such as tz-, ov-, and cvx- to separate documentation, overview mock, and component examples.
Keeps specimen data in JavaScript arrays and generates repeated registry/legend rows from those arrays.
Uses inline style custom properties for per-instance zone coloring in the overview tiles.
Uses text/babel for the React tweak UI, implying browser-side JSX compilation for this handoff artifact.
concerns: The file depends on local CSS and tweaks-panel.jsx plus external CDN scripts, so the interactive page is not self-contained.
Registry token rows are assigned through innerHTML, which is acceptable for static literals here but couples markup construction to string templates.
The React/Babel setup is development-oriented and adds runtime weight for a small controls panel.
Several visual meanings depend on CSS variables defined outside this file, so the HTML alone does not fully specify the rendered result.
notes: A handoff/specimen page that documents and demonstrates ConceptV tonal surface zones with generated examples and adjustable visual parameters.
questions: Are the CDN-hosted React and Babel scripts intended for the final handoff environment or just local specimen viewing?
Should the generated TZ_ZONES data be reused elsewhere as a source of design-token metadata?
Are the warm/cooler/warmer palette modes documented outside this page?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents a surface-zone language, documents semantic zones, and demonstrates those zones in component and dashboard contexts.
apparent_themes: Vi as decision and recommendation accent
intention_signals: The file appears to translate an abstract tonal-zoning design principle into a visible handoff artifact with examples, token references, and adjustable parameters.
novelty: A design-system page treats subtle surface undertones as a named semantic language, with pigment percentages, a global intensity knob, and an applied dashboard mock in the same artifact.
contribution: For a fused system, this file contributes a semantic tonal-surface vocabulary, generated registry structure, adjustable tint/gold/spatial controls, and an applied example showing how zones map to interface roles.
summary_for_embedding: Static ConceptV design-system HTML specimen for tonal zoning, with generated semantic surface registry, component role demos, applied Overview dashboard mock, legend, responsive scaling, and React tweak controls.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for briefing and testing how ConceptV should use subtle tinted surfaces, gold accents, and spatial lines across a product interface.
